title: Orchestrator Prompt — Phase 2: Isolated Builds
what_it_is: The live operating prompt for a fresh orchestrator session running Phase 2 ("Isolated Builds") of the 4-phase Orvex Studio Delivery Program. It layers exactly three Phase-2 deltas — Rule 1 contract-first dispatch (a story is frontier-eligible only once its service's contract tag lands and meets/exceeds its Definition Pack's named tag), Rule 2 isolation doctrine (siblings faked from contracts golden fixtures; crew-slot.sh narrow live checks; crew-up.sh whole-family checks), and Rule 3 continuous proving (nightly six-surface family-E2E + a merge-freeze ratchet on red) — onto the UNCHANGED Act-3 Delivery Orchestrator loop (gkkUDzn277 §6A). It also carries Stage 0-6 gates, the Stage-5 UI surface rewrite waves (0-6), Linear/capacity/fake-done/escalation protocol sections, and a large embedded User-Behavior-Analytics (PostHog) build spec as Stage-6-style DoD detail.
overlaps: Correctly layers on gkkUDzn277 (Act-3 machinery, reused verbatim) and correctly cites 5eFdxN3edd (program plan) as parent — no conflict there. The real overlap is with the NEW local parallel-build-plan.md (8-teams-by-service, contract-tag unlock): that doc's central claim ('the contract tag is the one unlock — parallelism unlocks service-by-service as each tag lands, never wait-for-all-then-go') is ALREADY this page's Rule 1 near-verbatim ('the contract tag ... is the build authorization; no story dispatches against an untagged contract'). So parallel-build-plan.md is NOT new doctrine and must not become a competing/duplicate build-plan page — that is exactly the confusion risk the PO flagged. Its genuinely net-new content is the STAFFING/PARTITION overlay this page lacks: a named Platform team + 8 domain-affinity service teams (Identity&Workflows, Knowledge&AI, Product BFF&Billing, Wiki engine, Agent surfaces, Staging&Workgraph, Human surfaces) + a standing Integration team, plus the cross-team contract-handshake map and load-balancing note. This page's existing 'Capacity' section (floor ~15/ceiling 32, two engines over disjoint partitions) is the natural anchor for that — the team names ARE a concrete instance of 'disjoint project partitions.' Recommend: fold parallel-build-plan.md's Phase A/B/C team-allocation content into this page's Capacity section (as a named-partition appendix) and cross-link Rule 1 to it, rather than publishing it as a standalone wiki page — do not duplicate the contract-tag-unlock explanation, which already lives here.
